page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-180 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It features 180 vCPUs mapped to 90 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 354.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 1.97 GB of memory per core. It operates on the x86_64 architecture and includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, including VNNI and BF16, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory subsystem performance is also highly rated, with uncached read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks reaching 346.92 GB/sec and 173.38 GB/sec respectively. Single-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests. The hardware demonstrates top-tier efficiency in specialized tasks, including software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations.

Given its high compute density and low memory-to-core ratio, the c3d-highcpu-180 is optimized for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is highly suited for large-scale software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression tasks. Additionally, the processor's AVX-512 support enables efficient execution of large language model inference, particularly for prompt processing and text generation on medium to large models. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs makes it a cost-efficient choice for pure compute scaling where external storage is utilized.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-180 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It delivers 180 vCPUs across 90 physical cores, paired with 354.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a lean 1.97 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, focusing resources entirely on raw processing power. It features advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and cryptographic accelerators. Benchmark results place this server in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. It also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in software compilation and LLM inference. This configuration is highly efficient for CPU-intensive, parallelizable workloads such as static web serving, ray tracing, and large-scale data processing that require high compute density without massive memory overhead.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-180 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 180 vCPUs, 354 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC 9B14 processors, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for CPU-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-180 is a compute-optimized server featuring 180 vCPUs, 354 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors for high-density workloads.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server delivering top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for parallel processing and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors with 180 vCPUs and 354 GB RAM
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth
Supports AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix operations
No bundled local storage or GPUs, optimizing cost for pure compute tasks
Best suited for software compilation, static web serving, and LLM inference
